summary: Iter22 Landing rebuild verified end-to-end. All 7 sections render in the exact required top-to-bottom order with correct testids and copy. Before/After uses identical SHARED_IMG src (photo-1564501049412-61c2a3083791) with the two specified CSS filter treatments. Financial transition rows ft-adr/ft-occ/ft-rev render with strikethrough + arrow + animated child. Why-different has 3 columns x 4 list items. Workflow has 6 cells flow-0..5 numbered 01-06. All 13 OLD testids (before-after-section, gap-section, inevitability-section, revenue-strip, ba-before/after, rs-adr/occ/rev, workflow-0..3) are correctly removed (count=0). Regression sweep on /method, /pricing, /portfolio/demo, /dashboard/demo, /workspace/demo/0/mezzanine_loft, /visualize/demo/0 all clean — body bg #F2EAD8 preserved everywhere.
critical_code_review_comments: Landing.jsx imports BarChart3 and Layers from lucide-react but never uses them — dead imports (line 2). Minor cleanup; harmless but flagged by no-unused-vars-style hygiene.

Workflow cell renders an empty <span className='hidden md:block absolute' /> when i < FLOW.length-1 (line 306-308) — this absolute span has no content/child and no positioning context. Either remove it or wire the connector arrow it appears to be intended for.
Single-file Landing.jsx is fine at 421 lines (under 700-line threshold). Helpers CaseFrame/Transition/DarkStat are well-isolated.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (83/83 effective checks; 8 reported 'fails' were CSS text-transform:uppercase artifacts in inner_text — DOM content matches spec exactly)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing testid cohort for Iter22: hero-image, hero-title, hero-sub, hero-metric, hero-cta-analyze, hero-cta-demo, revenue-section, case-study-section, financial-transition, ft-adr/ft-occ/ft-rev (+ -anim children), case-before, case-after (both share same img src, differ by inline style filter), how-section, step-0..2, why-different-section, why-col-generic-ai, why-col-traditional-tools, why-col-vela, workflow-section, workflow-flow, flow-0..5, final-cta-section, cta-analyze-bottom, cta-demo-bottom. When asserting kicker / source-label / ft-row label text via inner_text(), remember CSS uppercase transform — compare case-insensitive or use textContent of the inner element directly. /visualize/demo/0 keeps polling for AI image gen so use wait_until='domcontentloaded' instead of 'networkidle'.
rca of the issue: No issues found. The 8 surface-level test failures during my run were tester-side artifacts: kickers (.kicker, .kicker-bronze) and .source-label use text-transform:uppercase, so Playwright inner_text() returns uppercased characters even though the DOM source matches the spec exactly ('Revenue Intelligence', 'Case Study', 'How VELA Works', 'Why VELA Is Different', 'Transformation Workflow', 'Benchmark · Mediterranean boutique comp-set', 'Occupancy', 'Annual Revenue'). All semantic content is correct — confirmed by reading Landing.jsx source directly (lines 103, 131, 159, 173-174, 197, 234, 282).
